_notice: ALL RECORDS BELOW ARE FICTIONAL. This file illustrates the STRUCTURE and RELATIONSHIPS of the grants/outcomes model. It contains no production data. Every name, number, date, identifier, and value was invented for demonstration and must not be interpreted as real. No record resembles a real guest, client, employee, donor, or funder.
_evidence_tier: Invented sample. Entity and field names reconstruct the verified model; values are fictional.
_model_reference: See measurement-model.md and data-dictionary.csv for the entities used here.

theory_of_change.name: Pathways to Stable Housing (SAMPLE)
theory_of_change.summary: Fictional theory of change: coordinated shelter, case management, and employment support reduce return-to-homelessness.

award.note: Fictional figures. 'requested' and 'awarded' are shown separately on purpose: a request is not an award, and dollar figures are grant-specific and never interchangeable between grants.

indicators.name: Percent of exits to permanent housing (SAMPLE)
indicators._note: Illustrative KPI only. The count of deployed msnfp_indicator records is pending verification; this is not a stated total.

measurement_items.definition: Exits to permanent housing / total exits, per reporting period (SAMPLE)

program_activities.name: Weekly case-management sessions delivered (SAMPLE)
program_activities.note: Fictional program-activity record showing how delivered activity feeds a measure.

program_activities.name: Behavioral-health assessment completed (SAMPLE, generalized)
program_activities._privacy_note: Behavioral-health example is intentionally GENERALIZED. The exact clinical instrument/schema name is withheld, consistent with the public catalog's clinical-name sanitization.

results.narrative: Fictional interim result: 58% of exits were to permanent housing against a 60% target; 96 of 175 targeted individuals served.

evidence.note: Fictional evidence pointer. Demonstrates audit-ready lineage: a reported result cites the supporting evidence type and record.

survey_instrument_example.note: Fictional intake survey record used only to show survey-to-measure linkage.
